reasoning: The base rate for an album spending 17 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 in the modern era is near zero (the last to achieve this was Billy Ray Cyrus in 1992). The album in question, Taylor Swift's 'The Life of a Showgirl', faces insurmountable obstacles: it has only 12 tracks (limiting long-term streaming numbers compared to her previous 31-track TTPD, which managed 12 consecutive weeks), and its weekly units have already declined to 194,000 by week 3. To reach 17 straight weeks, it would have to dominate the chart through early February 2026, forcing it to survive the massive streaming surges of holiday music throughout December, as well as potential major blockbuster releases from artists like Drake. Given these factors, it is highly unlikely to achieve 17 consecutive weeks.
